output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 7/7.
Batch 1: Staged the `moderation_decisions` migration and updated the staging handoff README. Verified the new SQL file exists with the exact six-value classification CHECK constraint, all three required indexes, and the documented deploy destination. Wrote a batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/design-and-implement-a-20260419-1105/execution_batch_1.json`.
Batch 2: Added the three `DatabaseHandler` helpers required for moderation-decision logging in `src/common/db_handler.py` and verified the new block directly. `record_moderation_decision` is fail-closed on `_gate_check(guild_id)`, uses the same fluent Supabase insert + try/except pattern as `log_reaction_event`, and the two read helpers return the requested row shapes from `discord_reactions` and `discord_messages`. Ran `python -m py_compile src/common/db_handler.py` as a module-level syntax check and wrote the batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/design-and-implement-a-20260419-1105/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Updated `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` for the restricted-emoji moderation path: the cog now tracks short-lived bot-initiated removal markers, classifies restricted emoji into `flag`/`political`/`religious`, registers the marker before `remove_reaction`, and writes one `bot_auto_restricted` moderation decision after a successful removal with `reason=category` and a 200-character message snippet. Verified `_is_restricted_emoji` no longer exists in `src/`, the new methods are present, the ordering is correct, and `python -m py_compile src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` passes. Wrote the batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/design-and-implement-a-20260419-1105/execution_batch_3.json`.
Batch 4: Implemented the marker-consume path in `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` and the curator marker registrations in `src/features/curating/curator.py`. `on_raw_reaction_remove` now pops and consumes bot-initiated markers before any self-removal write, records `user_self_removal` only when no marker exists, marks suspicion only for `🤮`/`👎`/`😭`, and sends the admin DM in a fire-and-forget task with its own try/except. The four curator `❌` reaction-removal sites now register `bot_curator_reject` markers immediately before removal, with no direct moderation-decision write added to curator. Verified both modules with `python -m py_compile` and wrote the batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/design-and-implement-a-20260419-1105/execution_batch_4.json`.
Batch 5: Added the three DB-snapshot listeners to `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py`: `on_raw_reaction_clear`, `on_raw_reaction_clear_emoji`, and `on_raw_message_delete`. Each path enumerates `get_active_reactors(...)`, writes one moderation-decision row per active reactor with the correct classification, and keeps `is_suspicious=False`. `on_raw_message_delete` reads `get_message_snapshot(payload.message_id)` at handler entry before any await, so it stays ahead of LoggerCog’s soft-delete path and falls back to null author/content fields when the snapshot is unavailable. Verified the module with `python -m py_compile src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` and wrote the batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/design-and-implement-a-20260419-1105/execution_batch_5.json`.
Batch 6: Created `tests/test_moderation_decisions.py` using `conftest.load_module_from_repo` to load both `reactor_cog.py` and `db_handler.py`, with a mocked Supabase-backed `DatabaseHandler` instance and monkeypatched `record_moderation_decision`, `get_active_reactors`, `get_message_snapshot`, and `bot.fetch_user` surfaces inside the test harness. The module covers the nine requested scenario groups: restricted flag/political/religious add path plus gateway no-op expectation, curator marker path, plain self-removal, suspicious self-removal admin DM, clear-all, clear-emoji filter, message-delete cascade, `_classify_restricted_emoji` branches, and TTL pruning via monkeypatched `time.monotonic`. I syntax-checked the file with `python -m py_compile tests/test_moderation_decisions.py`; the full module run is deferred to T8 per the plan.
Batch 7: Validation completed. `pytest tests/test_moderation_decisions.py -q` initially exposed one real regression: `on_raw_reaction_remove` was writing a second `bot_auto_restricted` row after the restricted-emoji branch had already logged eagerly. I fixed that by making `bot_auto_restricted` markers suppression-only in the remove handler, then reran the targeted module to green (`20 passed`). After that, `pytest -q` passed cleanly (`241 passed, 3 warnings`). The throwaway `/tmp/smoke_moderation.py` script confirmed the suspicious `🤮` self-retract path end-to-end (`smoke-ok`) and was deleted. I also updated `.megaplan/plans/design-and-implement-a-20260419-1105/final.md` so the PR-body draft explicitly says `.migrations_staging/20260419110000_create_moderation_decisions.sql` must be copied into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` before deploy, and it now includes the remaining manual Discord smoke checklist.

task_updates.executor_notes: Created `.migrations_staging/20260419110000_create_moderation_decisions.sql` with the required `moderation_decisions` schema, exact six-value classification CHECK constraint, and the three requested indexes. Updated `.migrations_staging/README.md` to include the new staged migration and the manual handoff destination `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/`.

Added `record_moderation_decision`, `get_active_reactors`, and `get_message_snapshot` near `log_reaction_event` in `src/common/db_handler.py`. Verified `record_moderation_decision` begins with `_gate_check(guild_id)`, uses the same fluent-client insert + try/except structure as `log_reaction_event`, `get_active_reactors` reads `user_id`/`emoji`/`guild_id` from `discord_reactions` with `removed_at IS NULL` and optional emoji filtering, and `get_message_snapshot` returns `author_id`/`author_name`/`content`/`channel_id`/`guild_id` from `discord_messages`.

Updated `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` to initialize `_bot_initiated_removals`, add `_prune_bot_removals` and `register_bot_removal`, refactor `_is_restricted_emoji` into `_classify_restricted_emoji`, and wire the restricted-emoji branch to register a bot-removal marker before `remove_reaction` and write exactly one `bot_auto_restricted` moderation decision after a successful removal. Verified the moderation row uses `reason=category` and truncates `message_content_snippet` to 200 chars; confirmed no `_is_restricted_emoji` references remain in `src/` and the module passes `py_compile`.

Updated `on_raw_reaction_remove` in `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` to pop and consume `_bot_initiated_removals[(message.id, user.id, emoji_str)]` before any self-removal write, emitting exactly one moderation-decision row from the marker path and returning immediately so no duplicate `user_self_removal` row is written. Verified the no-marker path writes `classification='user_self_removal'`, sets `is_suspicious` only for `🤮`/`👎`/`😭`, and triggers an admin DM via a fire-and-forget task whose try/except is separate from the DB write path; the DM body includes the reactor mention, emoji, `message.jump_url`, and the 200-character snippet.

Inserted the curator marker-registration block immediately before all four `await message.remove_reaction('❌', user)` sites in `src/features/curating/curator.py` and verified there are exactly four such registrations. No direct `record_moderation_decision` call was added to curator; the remove handler in `ReactorCog` remains the sole writer for the curator path. Minor implementation deviation: `ArtCurator` is itself a bot subclass and has no `self.bot`, so the insertion uses `self.get_cog('ReactorCog')` rather than `self.bot.get_cog(...)`.

Added `on_raw_reaction_clear`, `on_raw_reaction_clear_emoji`, and `on_raw_message_delete` listeners to `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py`. Verified each path enumerates `db_handler.get_active_reactors(...)`, writes one moderation-decision row per active reactor with the requested classification, and sets `is_suspicious=False`; `on_raw_message_delete` reads `get_message_snapshot(payload.message_id)` at the top of the handler before any await, then uses DB snapshots only with graceful null fallbacks when snapshot data is missing.

Created `tests/test_moderation_decisions.py` using `conftest.load_module_from_repo` to load both `reactor_cog.py` and `db_handler.py`, with a mocked Supabase-backed `DatabaseHandler` instance and monkeypatched `record_moderation_decision`, `get_active_reactors`, `get_message_snapshot`, and `bot.fetch_user` surfaces inside the test harness. The module covers the nine requested scenario groups: restricted flag/political/religious add path plus gateway no-op expectation, curator marker path, plain self-removal, suspicious self-removal admin DM, clear-all, clear-emoji filter, message-delete cascade, `_classify_restricted_emoji` branches, and TTL pruning via monkeypatched `time.monotonic`; syntax-checked with `python -m py_compile tests/test_moderation_decisions.py`.

Ran `pytest tests/test_moderation_decisions.py -q` and fixed the one implementation regression it exposed: `on_raw_reaction_remove` was double-writing `bot_auto_restricted` after the restricted-emoji branch had already written eagerly, so marker consumption for `bot_auto_restricted` is now suppression-only. Re-ran the targeted module to green (`20 passed`), then ran the full suite to green (`241 passed`). Wrote and ran the throwaway `/tmp/smoke_moderation.py` script to confirm the suspicious `🤮` self-retract path emits exactly one `user_self_removal` decision with `is_suspicious=True` and one admin DM send (`smoke-ok`), then deleted the script. Updated `.megaplan/plans/design-and-implement-a-20260419-1105/final.md` with the manual migration handoff note for `.migrations_staging/20260419110000_create_moderation_decisions.sql` and the required manual Discord smoke checklist. Baseline `pytest -q --tb=no` could not be captured before edits because the code changes were already in progress when T8 began.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed the staged SQL exists under `.migrations_staging/` with the `20260419110000_` prefix, the CHECK constraint enumerates exactly `bot_auto_restricted`, `bot_curator_reject`, `user_self_removal`, `moderator_cleared_all`, `moderator_cleared_emoji`, and `message_deleted_cascade`, all three indexes are present, and `.migrations_staging/README.md` was updated.

Confirmed `record_moderation_decision`, `get_active_reactors`, and `get_message_snapshot` now exist in `src/common/db_handler.py`. `record_moderation_decision` calls `_gate_check(guild_id)` as its first statement and follows the same try/except write pattern as `log_reaction_event`; the read helpers use the fluent Supabase client only and return the documented dict shapes.

Confirmed `ReactorCog.__init__` initializes `self._bot_initiated_removals = {}`, `register_bot_removal` and `_prune_bot_removals` exist and pruning runs on every registration, `_classify_restricted_emoji` returns `flag`/`political`/`religious`/`None`, the old `_is_restricted_emoji` reference is gone, and the restricted-emoji branch registers the marker before `await message.remove_reaction(...)` then writes one `bot_auto_restricted` moderation decision with `reason=category` and a 200-character snippet.

Confirmed the marker-present branch in `on_raw_reaction_remove` pops the marker, writes exactly one moderation-decision row with the marker classification/reason, and returns before any `user_self_removal` write. The no-marker branch writes `user_self_removal`, sets `is_suspicious` only for `🤮`/`👎`/`😭`, and sends the admin DM in a separate fire-and-forget task wrapped in its own try/except with reactor mention, emoji, jump URL, and the 200-char snippet.

Verified all four curator `await message.remove_reaction('❌', user)` sites are immediately preceded by `register_bot_removal(..., 'bot_curator_reject', reason='curator_reject')`, and no direct `record_moderation_decision` call was added in `curator.py`. Because `ArtCurator` has no `self.bot` attribute, the safe local equivalent is `self.get_cog('ReactorCog')`.

Confirmed the three new listeners are registered in `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py`. Each one uses `get_active_reactors` and writes one row per reactor with `classification` set to `moderator_cleared_all`, `moderator_cleared_emoji`, or `message_deleted_cascade` respectively, with `is_suspicious=False` on all three paths. `on_raw_message_delete` reads the message snapshot synchronously at handler entry before any await, so it stays ahead of LoggerCog's soft-delete race; when `get_active_reactors` returns an empty list, the handlers no-op.

Confirmed `tests/test_moderation_decisions.py` covers the requested scenario set: restricted flag/political/religious add with correct reasons and gateway no-op expectation, curator marker consumption without duplicate `user_self_removal`, plain self-removal with no admin DM, suspicious self-removal for `🤮`/`👎`/`😭` with admin DM payload assertions, clear-all enumeration, clear-emoji filtering, message-delete cascade snapshot population, `_classify_restricted_emoji` branch coverage, and TTL pruning via monkeypatched `time.monotonic`. The assertions check exact moderation-decision kwargs such as `classification`, `reason`, and `is_suspicious`, not only call counts.

Confirmed `pytest tests/test_moderation_decisions.py -q` passed after fixing the duplicate `bot_auto_restricted` write on gateway remove, and `pytest -q` passed with no regressions (`241 passed`). The throwaway `/tmp/smoke_moderation.py` script confirmed the `🤮` self-retract admin-DM path (`smoke-ok`) and was then deleted. `.megaplan/plans/design-and-implement-a-20260419-1105/final.md` now explicitly notes that `.migrations_staging/20260419110000_create_moderation_decisions.sql` must be copied into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` before deploy.
